commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and API/session-delivery compatibility judgment, not a narrow automated code edit.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff does not change dependencies, workflows, auth, secrets, or external code execution, and the new cleanup path is workspace-bounded. (sha=cd4e345a12e274ddcbf53f82c1c06c76121fa0ce)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Gateway chat.send/chat.abort cancellation timing and terminal ACK handling, node subscription session-key canonicalization, Control UI lifecycle reconciliation, the forwarded skills revision ACK schema, docs, and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +343, Tests +1400, Docs 0. Total +1743 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main registers the chat abort controller only after awaited attachment/model preprocessing, so an immediate chat.abort by runId can miss the active send. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and API/session-delivery compatibility judgment, not a narrow automated code edit.

target.title: Fix chat.abort during attachment send preprocessing
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the `chat.abort` / `chat.send` race from #84176 by registering the chat run abort controller **before** awaited attachment/model preprocessing, so an immediate abort can find and cancel the just-started run.
- An aborted-before/at-dispatch send now acks terminal `{runId,status:"timeout"}` instead of non-terminal `{status:"started"}`. This prevents the Control UI and `agent.wait` from treating an already-cancelled run as still active.
- Post-dispatch abort detection reads **this run's own AbortController signal**, not the shared `chatAbortedRuns` marker, so stale or cross-run same-key abort markers no longer force a fresh run to report `timeout` or swallow its real error. The shared marker remains in place for existing abort-tail suppression.
- Terminal non-OK `chat.send` ACKs now clear the Control UI's optimistic local run/stream state through the shared run lifecycle reconciler, including the queued app-chat path. New visible sends clear stale local-terminal suppression state.
- Scope boundary: this widens the `chat.send` ACK `status` union to include terminal `"timeout"`/`"error"` values in the Control UI ACK normalizer and forwarded skills-revision ACK schema. No new RPC method, config/auth/security/network change, or channel adapter change.

AI-assisted; contributor manually reviewed and is responsible for the change.

## Linked context

- Closes #84176
- Related #84274
- Current candidate/proof head for this update: `cd4e345a12e274ddcbf53f82c1c06c76121fa0ce`
- Current upstream/main checked locally: `9313471fa579eec9da1814fac9f0be32e148e666`; merge-tree with this branch was clean.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed:

`chat.abort` called immediately after an attachment `chat.send` now cancels the in-preprocess run, and the send returns terminal `{runId,status:"timeout"}` rather than a non-terminal `started` ACK. A post-dispatch abort also leaves terminal dedupe for duplicate same-key sends / `agent.wait`. A fresh same-key send after an older abort is reported from its own run signal, not from a stale shared abort marker. The Control UI clears local optimistic run/stream state when `chat.send` itself returns terminal `timeout` or `error`, including the queued send path.

- Real environment tested:

Current head `cd4e345a12` was exercised in the gateway integration harness and the Control UI workspace tests. The post-dispatch abort proof runs over a real Gateway loopback WebSocket with the production `chat.send` and `chat.abort` handlers; the agent model reply is stubbed so no provider call is made. The central pre-dispatch attachment-abort proof drives the real `chat.send` / `chat.abort` handlers with a real image attachment and a controlled pending model-catalog promise to hold attachment preprocessing open; it asserts no dispatch and no chat-run registration occur. The UI idle proof drives the real Control UI controller/app-chat send path and lifecycle reconciler; it asserts the local active run, stream text, stream timestamp, stale-active suppression, and refresh queue state after terminal ACKs.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
# Current-head gateway proof: pre-dispatch attachment abort handler path +
# post-dispatch loopback ws abort/dedupe smoke.
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts \
  -t "chat.abort stops an attachment send while preprocessing is still pending|smoke: supports abort and idempotent completion" \
  --reporter=dot

# Current-head Control UI lifecycle proof.
( cd ui && node ../node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run \
    src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts \
    src/ui/app-chat.test.ts \
    src/ui/chat/run-lifecycle.test.ts \
    --reporter=dot )

# Current-head validation after the UI terminal-ACK fix.
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json \
  --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/chat/run-lifecycle.ts
git diff --check a3992f4e620cb00bb0fe705689dbfeb71033f96a..HEAD
```

- Evidence after fix:

Current-head focused proof runs:

```text
Gateway focused proof on cd4e345a12:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       2 passed | 35 skipped (37)

Control UI focused proof on cd4e345a12:
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       222 passed (222)
```

The committed pre-dispatch attachment-abort regression holds preprocessing pending, aborts the same run id, then releases preprocessing. It asserts the production handler returns the terminal ACK and does not dispatch or register a chat run:

```json
{
  "scenario": "chat.abort stops an attachment send while preprocessing is still pending",
  "sendParams": {
    "sessionKey": "main",
    "message": "see image",
    "idempotencyKey": "idem-abort-attachment-race",
    "attachments": [{ "type": "image", "mimeType": "image/png", "fileName": "dot.png" }]
  },
  "abort": {
    "ok": true,
    "aborted": true,
    "runIds": ["idem-abort-attachment-race"]
  },
  "sendAckAfterPreprocessingReleased": {
    "runId": "idem-abort-attachment-race",
    "status": "timeout"
  },
  "dispatchInboundMessageCalled": false,
  "addChatRunCalled": false,
  "removeChatRunArgs": [
    "idem-abort-attachment-race",
    "idem-abort-attachment-race",
    "agent:main:main"
  ]
}
```

Live post-dispatch payloads previously captured from the real Gateway loopback WebSocket path remain covered by the current-head smoke rerun. The first send dispatches, abort cancels it, and duplicate same-key send observes terminal `timeout` instead of a stuck non-terminal `started`:

```json
{
  "scenario": "chat.abort immediately after chat.send, real Gateway over loopback ws",
  "sendAck": { "runId": "idem-proof", "status": "started" },
  "abort": { "ok": true, "aborted": true, "runIds": ["idem-proof"] },
  "duplicateSendAfterAbort_observesTerminalTimeout": {
    "runId": "idem-proof",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
    "status": "timeout",
    "summary": "aborted",
    "stopReason": "rpc"
  },
  "historyMessageCount": 0,
  "secondAbortAfterSettle": { "ok": true, "aborted": false, "runIds": [] }
}
```

The Control UI terminal-ACK tests assert the visible local run state returns idle instead of staying stuck active:

```json
{
  "directChatSendTerminalTimeout": {
    "ack": { "runId": "<same generated idempotency key>", "status": "timeout" },
    "chatRunId": null,
    "chatStream": null,
    "chatStreamStartedAt": null,
    "chatRunStatus": { "phase": "interrupted" },
    "lastLocalTerminalReconcile": { "sessionStatus": "killed" }
  },
  "directChatSendTerminalError": {
    "chatRunId": null,
    "chatStream": null,
    "chatStreamStartedAt": null,
    "lastLocalTerminalReconcile": { "sessionStatus": "failed" }
  },
  "queuedRefreshingTerminalTimeout": {
    "chatRunId": null,
    "chatStream": null,
    "chatStreamStartedAt": null,
    "refreshSessionsAfterChatSize": 0
  },
  "mismatchedTerminalAck": {
    "chatRunId": null,
    "lastLocalTerminalReconcile": null
  }
}
```

Supplemental validation after the final UI commit: UI `tsgo` exit 0, `oxfmt --check` clean, `git diff --check` clean, and all PR-range commits verify with the expected noreply identity and SSH signatures.

- Observed result after fix:

Pre-dispatch attachment abort returns terminal `{runId,status:"timeout"}` from `chat.send`, does not call the dispatch path, and does not register a live chat run. Post-dispatch abort leaves terminal dedupe so duplicate same-key sends and waiters observe `timeout`. The Control UI treats terminal ACKs as finished/interrupted lifecycle events: local `chatRunId`, stream buffer, and stream timestamp are cleared, queued refresh bookkeeping is not leaked, and stale-active row suppression is armed only for same-run terminal ACKs.

- What was not tested:

No real provider call is made in the abort proofs; the post-dispatch loopback run stubs the agent reply and the pre-dispatch proof intentionally cancels before dispatch. The pre-dispatch attachment-abort proof uses a controlled pending catalog promise to hold preprocessing open, rather than a full loopback WebSocket capture for that exact branch. Not tested: manual browser screenshot/recording, remote/non-loopback Gateway, or real model-provider dispatch after preprocessing.

## Known trade-offs / out of scope

- The shared abort marker still conflates "this run id was aborted" suppression with same-id liveness. This PR fixes stale-marker post-dispatch decisions with the per-run controller signal while preserving existing abort-tail suppression; a marker generation/epoch refactor is separate scope.
- Same-key idempotency reuse remains inherently ambiguous during rare concurrent retries. Existing sticky cancellation dedupe can keep `chat:<key>` terminal after an earlier same-key cancellation; this PR does not change that broader dedupe behavior.
- Hoisting terminal dedupe writes into `abortChatRunById` could close a theoretical never-settles path, but that is larger than the targeted #84176 fix.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Immediate abort now reaches in-preprocess sends, aborted sends ACK terminal `timeout`, and terminal ACKs clear the Control UI local active run.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No

What is the highest-risk area?

Cancellation/idempotency timing around early abort registration, post-dispatch abort detection, dedupe caching, and Control UI optimistic run lifecycle.

How is that risk mitigated?

Abort detection uses each run's own AbortController signal, while the shared abort marker is preserved for abort-tail suppression. Non-aborted sends keep their existing `started` / `in_flight` ACK behavior. Focused gateway and UI regressions cover pre-dispatch attachment abort, post-dispatch abort terminal dedupe, stale-marker same-key behavior, terminal ACK UI clearing, queued app-chat clearing, and widened ACK schema acceptance.

## Tests and validation

Commands run on current candidate head `cd4e345a12e274ddcbf53f82c1c06c76121fa0ce`:

- Gateway focused proof (`server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b`, pre-dispatch attachment abort + loopback smoke) --- 2 tests passed.
- Control UI focused proof (`controllers/chat`, `app-chat`, `run-lifecycle`) --- 222 tests passed.
- UI `tsgo` typecheck --- exit 0.
- `oxfmt --check` over changed UI files --- clean.
- `git diff --check a3992f4e62..HEAD` --- clean.
- Commit identity/signature over the PR range --- expected noreply identity and SSH signatures.

Regression coverage added/updated in this PR:

- abort-before/at-dispatch attachment `chat.send` acks terminal `timeout` and writes terminal abort dedupe; duplicate same-key send observes terminal `timeout`.
- post-dispatch abort uses the run's own controller signal, so stale shared same-key abort markers no longer misclassify a fresh run.
- Control UI terminal `timeout` / `error` ACKs clear local optimistic run/stream state; queued app-chat does the same and does not leak deferred refresh bookkeeping.
- `normalizeChatSendAck` preserves terminal statuses; forwarded skills-revision ACK schema accepts the widened statuses and rejects unknown ones.

## Current review state

Candidate head `cd4e345a12e274ddcbf53f82c1c06c76121fa0ce` is prepared locally but not yet pushed. This update refreshes the proof text for the pre-dispatch attachment-abort path and the terminal ACK UI idle fix. Waiting on approval to push the prepared commit and update the PR body.
target.author: nxmxbbd

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
